# chromonym

> The last color-naming library you'll ever need. One API ‚Äî `identify` (color ‚Üí name), `resolve` (name ‚Üí color), `convert` (color ‚Üí color format) ‚Äî works against any palette matching `Palette<Name>`. Ships CSS, X11, Pantone; bring your own (brand colors, paint chips, game factions). Tree-shakes: you only pay for palettes you import. Not a general-purpose manipulation library ‚Äî for mixing / scales / gamut mapping, use chroma.js or color.js.

## Key files

- [README](./README.md) ‚Äî user-facing docs, API examples
- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) ‚Äî agent/contributor orientation, conventions
- [NOTICE.md](./NOTICE.md) ‚Äî Pantone trademark notice, upstream attribution

## Primary API

- `identify(input, { palette, metric })` ‚Äî color ‚Üí name. `palette` is a `Palette<Name>` object (defaults to `web`).
- `resolve(name, { palette, format })` ‚Äî name ‚Üí color. `palette` defaults to `web`.
- `convert(input, { format })` ‚Äî structural color-format conversion (HEX / RGB / RGBA / HSL / HSV). Intentionally does NOT handle PANTONE (palette-data dependent) ‚Äî use `pantoneToRgba` / `rgbaToPantone` from `chromonym/conversions/pantone`.
- `toRgba(input, knownFormat?)` / `fromRgba(rgba, format)` ‚Äî dispatcher primitives.
- Low-level converters: `hexToRgba`, `rgbaToHex`, `rgbToRgba`, `rgbaToRgb`, `hslToRgba`, `rgbaToHsl`, `hsvToRgba`, `rgbaToHsv`, `pantoneToRgba`, `rgbaToPantone`.
- Built-in palettes: `web`, `x11`, `pantone` (each a `Palette<Name>` object; `.colors` holds the hex map).

## BYO palettes

`identify` / `resolve` take a `Palette<Name>` object literal ‚Äî no registration. Shape:

```ts
type Palette<Name extends string = string> = {
  name: string;
  colors: Record<Name, HexColor>;
  normalize: (s: string) => string;
  defaultMetric: DistanceMetric;
};
```

## Conventions

- Format keys are SCREAMING_CAPS (`'HEX'`, `'RGB'`, etc.) ‚Äî dispatch keys.
- Palette identity is the object itself; there is no string-keyed registry.
- Canonical internal color: `Rgba = { r, g, b, a }`.
- Low-level converters throw on invalid input. High-level `identify` / `resolve` return `null`. `convert` throws.
- `sideEffects: false` ‚Äî no module-scope side effects anywhere.
- Test suite target: 100% line + function coverage across `src/`.
